title: Chapter 0: Product Overview
content:
# Tutor System Architecture

Tutor is an AI-powered learning interface for reading PDFs, asking a tutor questions, building a persistent learning library, and reviewing knowledge over time. The app combines a document reader, streaming chat, voice tutoring, live search, learner modeling, a 3D brain map, revision flashcards, analytics, admin tracing, and the `/brain` architecture cognition system.

## Core Surface

- **Study View**: PDF upload, document reading, annotation, usage analytics, and chat.
- **Chat Panel**: Streaming SSE tutor responses, web search, PDF page vision, graph updates, flashcard generation, markdown, Mermaid, code blocks, TTS, and voice.
- **Brain View**: A Three.js 3D graph of learner, learning books, and extracted concepts.
- **Revision View**: A paper-style library with the built-in Tutor System Architecture book, generated learning books, mapped concepts, notes, and flashcards.
- **Analytics View**: Dexie-backed concept, interaction, and session charts.
- **Admin View**: DeepSeek trace cards, learning book inspection, and live server console logs.

## Design System

The main product uses the Cosmic Obsidian theme: `#030303` and `#0A0A0B` backgrounds, neon violet/blue/orange accents, glass panels, motion transitions, and liquid UI details. Revision and Admin intentionally switch to a `#faf9f6` paper reading style so memory review feels like a notebook instead of a control panel.

title: Chapter 1: Runtime Stack And Navigation
content:
# Runtime Stack And Navigation

The frontend is React 19, Vite 6, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4, Zustand, `motion/react`, Dexie, `react-pdf`, `react-force-graph-3d`, Three.js, Recharts, React Markdown, Remark GFM, Mermaid, Shiki, and Lucide icons.

The backend is an Express server in `server.ts`. It uses the OpenAI SDK against OpenRouter, WebSockets through `ws`, Deepgram HTTP TTS, Deepgram Voice Agent proxying, and Serper web search/news retrieval.

## View Routing

The app has no URL router. `src/App.tsx` renders based on `activeView` from `src/store/index.ts`. Current views are:

- `study`
- `brain`
- `analytics`
- `revision`
- `admin`

`Navigation` exposes Study, Brain Map, Analytics, and Revision. Admin is available as a diagnostics surface and hides the normal navigation while active.

## State Ownership

Zustand owns API keys, learner name, active project, active learning book id, model selection, custom prompt, PDF state, annotations, selected text context, chat messages, voice status, web search events, source cache, pricing, and usage analytics. Local storage persists key user preferences and active learning context.

title: Chapter 2: Model Inventory
content:
# LLM And AI Model Inventory

Cloud LLM calls are brokered by `server.ts`. The app accepts a user OpenRouter key from Settings and falls back to `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` when available.

## OpenRouter Models

- **Default chat**: `deepseek/deepseek-chat`, labeled in the UI as DeepSeek V4 Flash.
- **Settings chat options**: `anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet`, `google/gemini-1.5-pro`, and `deepseek/deepseek-chat`.
- **Chat fallback chain**: `google/gemini-2.5-flash`, `anthropic/claude-3.5-haiku`, `openai/gpt-4o-mini`, and `meta-llama/llama-4-maverick`.
- **PDF title extraction**: `qwen/qwen2.5-vl-72b-instruct`.
- **Persona prompt generation**: `anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet`.
- **Trace explanations**: `deepseek/deepseek-chat`.
- **Learning book updates**: `deepseek/deepseek-chat`.
- **Flashcard extraction**: `deepseek/deepseek-chat`.
- **Page vision tool inside chat**: `openai/gpt-4o-mini`.

## Voice And Search Models

- **Deepgram Voice Agent listen**: `flux-general-en`.
- **Deepgram Voice Agent think**: `gpt-4o-mini` through Deepgram's OpenAI-compatible thinking provider.
- **Deepgram Voice Agent speak**: `aura-asteria-en`.
- **Read-aloud TTS**: default `aura-asteria-en`; Settings allows valid `aura-*-en` voices.
- **Web search**: Serper Google Search and Google News endpoints.

## Local Embeddings

Browser semantic memory uses `Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2`. The `/brain` retrieval index is also MiniLM-based and produces 384-dimensional chunks through `npm run brain:embed`.

## Pricing

`GET /api/pricing` fetches OpenRouter model pricing from the live OpenRouter models endpoint and caches it for six hours. Deepgram pricing is maintained as server constants for voice agent minutes, Aura character billing, and Flux listening estimates.

title: Chapter 3: Study, PDF, Chat, Search, And Voice
content:
# Study, PDF, Chat, Search, And Voice

## PDF Study Surface

`PdfViewer` renders PDFs with `react-pdf`. It supports zoom, fit-width, page navigation, highlights, underlines, strikethroughs, sticky notes, selected text capture, and normalized annotation rectangles. When a document page renders, the app can send a JPEG snapshot to `/api/title`, where Qwen vision generates a short project title and the memory orchestrator renames the current session learning book.

## Chat Panel

`ChatPanel` streams `/api/chat` responses through Server-Sent Events. It tracks phases such as retrieving, thinking, web search, synthesizing, and complete. It injects persistent memory, active learning book context, selected text context, custom system prompt, learner name, active project, and a current page image when the question appears visual.

Server-side tools are available to the LLM:

- `update_graph`: emit a concept update.
- `generate_flashcards`: return cards for Dexie storage.
- `web_search`: run Serper search or news.
- `look_at_current_page`: ask `openai/gpt-4o-mini` about the current PDF image.

## Search

Freshness-sensitive prompts and explicit web-search requests use Serper. Results are normalized into source cards with stable ids, domains, favicons, snippets, optional dates, canonical URLs, and positions. The server caches search results for 10 minutes.

## Voice

Voice mode captures microphone audio in the browser, converts it to PCM16 at 48 kHz, and streams it to `/api/voice-agent`. The server proxies to Deepgram Voice Agent and returns transcript events plus binary audio. Assistant voice responses are written into the same chat and learning-book memory flow. Read-aloud TTS uses `/api/tts` and streams Deepgram MP3 audio back to the browser.

title: Chapter 4: Learning Memory And Library
content:
# Learning Memory And Library

The persistent database is Dexie database `NeuralNestBrain` in `src/memory/longterm.memory.ts`. Version 6 contains:

- `concepts`: legacy and BKT-backed concepts.
- `misconceptions`: faulty mental models and resolution metadata.
- `sessions`: session start/end, pages visited, concepts, mistakes, and load level.
- `interactions`: user and assistant messages with optional embeddings.
- `flashcards`: active recall cards with next review timestamps.
- `traceLogs`: LLM explanations of internal actions.
- `learningBooks`: session learning books.
- `learningBookConcepts`: concepts extracted into books.
- `learningEntries`: conversation summaries, risks, model, and confidence.

## Memory Orchestrator

`MemoryOrchestrator` creates a new browser session, clears generated session learning library records, preserves the built-in Tutor System Architecture concept, creates a General Study learning book, and announces it through local storage plus `learning-book-updated`.

After chat or voice, it stores an embedded interaction, calls `/api/learning-book-update`, merges book/chapter/concept data into Dexie, writes a learning entry, updates active context, and optionally sends `/api/trace`.

## Session-Scoped Library

The generated library is intentionally session-scoped. Refreshing starts a clean generated learning book set, while the built-in Tutor System Architecture system book remains available unless hidden by the user. Chat context follows the selected `activeLearningBookId`, so future prompts include the active book overview, recent chapters, and mapped concepts.

title: Chapter 5: Learner Model
content:
# Learner Model

The Phase 5 learner model lives under `src/memory/` and is injected into prompts by `MemoryOrchestrator.getRelevantContext()`.

## Bayesian Knowledge Tracing

`BKTEngine` uses:

- `P(L0) = 0.2`
- `P(T) = 0.1`
- `P(S) = 0.1`
- `P(G) = 0.2`

Correct and incorrect attempts update `p_learn` with Bayes' theorem. Recognition attempts cap at `0.70`, generation attempts cap at `0.85`, and transfer attempts can rise toward `0.95`.

## ZPD And Scaffolding

`ZPDCalculator` divides concepts into:

- Independent: `p_learn >= 0.90`
- ZPD: `0.40 <= p_learn < 0.90`
- Not yet: `p_learn < 0.40`

A ZPD candidate is only ready when prerequisites are above threshold. `ScaffoldingEngine` calculates support from Level 5 full worked examples down to Level 0 independent work. Recent failed attempts increase support, and active misconceptions force at least Level 3.

## Cognitive Subsystems

- `MisconceptionGraph`: tracks active misconceptions and resolution strategy.
- `PrerequisiteDAG`: detects missing prerequisite knowledge.
- `IllusionDetector`: flags confidence more than `0.30` above performance.
- `CognitiveLoadMonitor`: recommends challenge, continue, simplify, or break.
- `ProductiveFailureEngine`: distinguishes productive struggle from destructive frustration.

The resulting tutor directives tell the model when to use Socratic correction, reinforce prerequisites, test transfer, reduce load, or interleave weak concepts.

title: Chapter 6: Brain, Revision, Analytics, And Admin
content:
# Brain, Revision, Analytics, And Admin

## Brain View

`BrainView` reads `learningBooks`, `learningBookConcepts`, and fallback `concepts` from Dexie. It builds a graph rooted at the learner, then links the learner to books, books to concepts, and concepts to child concepts. Nodes are rendered with Three.js through `react-force-graph-3d`. The view caches geometries, materials, textures, and sprite materials to avoid WebGL leaks.

## Revision View

`RevisionView` is the library and notebook surface. It shows the built-in Tutor System Architecture book, generated learning books, mapped concepts, recent notes, and flashcards. The book UI uses a paper background, serif type, Tailwind Typography, pattern cards, and long-press deletion for the built-in system card. Flashcards flip in 3D and reschedule based on Again, Hard, Good, and Easy.

## Analytics View

`AnalyticsView` reads concepts, interactions, and sessions from Dexie. It displays total concepts, interactions, study sessions, mastery bars, confidence bars, and mastery distribution with Recharts.

## Admin View

`AdminView` shows learning books, extracted concepts, chapters, risks, DeepSeek trace logs, and live server console logs. It connects to `/ws/debug`, where `server.ts` broadcasts recent console output and HTTP/backend activity.

title: Chapter 7: The Brain System For Agents
content:
# The `/brain` System For Agents

`/brain` is the repository's architecture cognition layer. It is generated from source and helps agents avoid broad repository reads.

## Required Commands

- `npm run brain:drift-check`: detect source drift.
- `npm run brain:generate`: regenerate graph, contracts, flows, impact, snapshots, retrieval chunks, and compressed context.
- `npm run brain:embed`: regenerate the semantic vector index.
- `npm run brain:retrieve -- "<task>"`: retrieve task-focused context.
- `npm run brain:impact -- "<file-or-symbol>"`: inspect blast radius.
- `npm run brain:verify`: validate architecture rules and freshness.
- `npm run brain:runtime-benchmark`: refresh runtime impact events.
- `npm run brain:self-audit`: score graph freshness, retrieval, runtime coverage, rules, loaders, and memory.
- `npm run brain:memory -- append ...`: record completed task memory.

## Main Artifacts

- `brain/knowledge/graph.json`
- `brain/contracts/api-contracts.json`
- `brain/flows/route-map.json`
- `brain/flows/state-flow.json`
- `brain/flows/render-graph.json`
- `brain/impact/impact-analysis.json`
- `brain/retrieval/vector-index.json`
- `brain/runtime/runtime-impact-map.json`
- `brain/snapshots/file-hashes.json`
- `brain/tasks/task-memory.json`

## Runtime Telemetry

When `VITE_BRAIN_RUNTIME=true` or local storage `brain_runtime=1`, `installBrainRuntime` instruments fetch, WebSocket, Zustand, Dexie, route changes, render profiling, and web search events. The runtime benchmark walks core surfaces so impact analysis can include observed behavior, not only static imports.

title: Chapter 8: Audit, Risks, And Maintenance Rules
content:
# Audit, Risks, And Maintenance Rules

## Current Audit Findings

- The current package stack is React 19, not React 18.
- Chat streams through `/api/chat` with SSE and model fallback.
- Default chat, trace, learning-book update, and flashcard generation use `deepseek/deepseek-chat`.
- PDF title extraction uses Qwen vision.
- Page vision inside chat uses `openai/gpt-4o-mini`.
- Voice uses Deepgram `flux-general-en`, `gpt-4o-mini`, and `aura-asteria-en`.
- Learning Library data is now based on `learningBooks`, `learningBookConcepts`, and `learningEntries`.
- Brain View prioritizes learning books and uses legacy concepts only as fallback.
- `/brain` includes graph generation, vector retrieval, runtime benchmark, drift check, verification, self-audit, and task memory.

## Known Risks

- Live OpenRouter, Deepgram, and Serper features require user or environment keys.
- Usage cost can begin as an estimate while streaming and be corrected when final provider usage arrives.
- Learning-book quality depends on OpenRouter availability; local fallback is intentionally simpler.
- `/brain` retrieval confidence calibration can remain low even when recall is high.
- Dexie schema, SSE contract, WebSocket contract, Zustand state, and ChatPanel tool parsing are high-impact mutation boundaries.

## Documentation Maintenance

Keep `TUTOR_ARCHITECTURE.md`, this in-app Tutor System Architecture book, and `brain/architecture.md` aligned with source. After editing documentation that affects retrieval, run `brain:generate`, `brain:embed`, `brain:runtime-benchmark`, `brain:verify`, `brain:drift-check`, and `brain:self-audit` before claiming the brain is current.
